channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What to know about Myanmar's natural disasters and civil war complicating relief efforts
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The Southeast Asian nation of Myanmar has been plagued by wars and natural disasters since it became independent from Britain in 1948, when it was still called Burma.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pope's willingness to show his frailty provides an example to young and old alike in aging world
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis’ frailty was on full view as he left Rome’s Gemelli hospital last Sunday after five weeks battling pneumonia that nearly killed him. He could barely lift his arms to bless the crowd. His eyes were sunken, face bloated.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2 people killed and dozens wounded in Russian attack on Kharkiv
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian drones hit a military hospital, shopping center, apartment blocks and other buildings in Kharkiv late Saturday, killing two people and wounding 35 others, Ukrainian officials said. Regional Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Transgender people are about 1% of the US population. Yet they're a political lightning rod
item.description: On the campaign trail, Donald Trump used contentiousness around transgender people's access to sports and bathrooms to fire up conservative voters and sway undecideds.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How a new Georgia bill could change the fate of domestic abuse survivors in prison
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Mary Favors is still plagued by nightmares from the days her husband beat her, choked her and verbally and sexually abused her. Now, she is in prison for killing him. Their fights often turned physical, and five times he was convicted of abusing her.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr. rallies Florida into Final Four with 84-79 March Madness win over Texas Tech
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Coach Todd Golden cut the final strand of the net, waved it to the crowd and performed the famed Gator Chomp. A celebration that seemed completely improbable just a few minutes earlier was in high gear. Florida is heading back to the Final Four. Walter Clayton Jr.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Deputies won't face charges in deadly 2022 shootout on California highway, prosecutors say
item.description: San Bernardino County sheriff's deputies involved in a 2022 shootout along a California freeway that killed a teenage girl and her fugitive father will not face any criminal charges, state Attorney General Rob Bonta announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas reaches 400 measles cases as US deals with outbreaks in 5 states
item.description: At least five states have active measles outbreaks as of Friday, and Texas' is the largest with 400 cases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UPS launching online tool to show tariff costs to customers
item.description: “Until now, international purchases often arrived with an unpleasant surprise – an additional bill for unpaid import costs.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, Fox 5

item.title: New wave of smaller, cheaper nuclear reactors sends US states racing to attract the industry
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — With the promise of newer, cheaper nuclear power on the horizon, U.S. states are vying to position themselves to build and supply the industry's next generation as policymakers consider expanding subsidies and paving over regulatory obstacles.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Delta plane, Air Force jet nearly crash in 'loss of separation' during Arlington National Cemetery flyover
item.description: A Delta plane carrying 131 passengers nearly collided with an Air Force jet in a 'loss of separation' Friday leading up to a flyover at Arlington National Cemetery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk says xAI buys social media platform X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday his artificial intelligence firm xAI has bought his social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Authorities find skeletal remains at home of Georgia man who died in Hawaii
item.description: Investigators at the medical examiner's office do not suspect any foul play, a representative told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Texas city almost entirely underwater amid rain storms
item.description: Nearly all of the city of Rio Hondo is underwater after the massive rain storms that hit the Rio Grande Valley on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Diana Eva Maldonado, NewsNation

item.title: Sevier County student hit while waiting for bus
item.description: SEVIER COUNTY, Tenn. (WATE) — A 10-year-old Sevier County Schools student was taken to the hospital after they were struck while waiting for the bus Friday morning, according to a school system spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: David Childs, architect of One World Trade Center that rose on Twin Towers site after 9/11, dies
item.description: David Childs, the lead architect of the One World Trade Center skyscraper that rose from the site where the Twin Towers collapsed in New York City during the 9/11 attacks, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wall Street tumbles on worries about slower economy, inflation
item.description: Another wipeout is swamping Wall Street Friday on worries about a potentially toxic mix of worsening inflation and a U.S. economy slowing because of households afraid to spend due to the global trade war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Baby's corpse, busted casket found on grounds of West Virginia cemetery
item.description: A baby's corpse and a busted casket were found at a cemetery in Salt Rock, West Virginia, on Thursday evening, according to the Cabell County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barren, Fox 5

item.title: Top-rated seafood restaurant in every state: Report
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: A look at who has been detained or deported in a US crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters
item.description: Supporters of Palestinian causes with ties to American universities have been detained in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A second South Carolina death row inmate chooses execution by firing squad
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina death row inmate on Friday chose execution by firing squad, just five weeks after the state carried out its first death by bullets. Mikal Mahdi, who pleaded guilty to murder for killing a police officer in 2004, is scheduled to be executed April 11.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate Republican chair challenges Trump over emergency funding move
item.description: Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine), the head of the powerful Senate Appropriations Committee, and her Democratic counterpart are challenging the Trump administration’s handling of emergency funding included in legislation passed earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Snake covered by Endangered Species Act killed at Michigan park
item.description: Officials are urging “understanding” over “fear” after a federally protected snake was recently found killed at the Matthaei Botanical Gardens and Nichols Arboretum in Ann Arbor.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KRON4

item.title: Waterbury woman pleads not guilty to holding stepson captive for decades
item.description: WATERBURY, Conn. (WTNH) — The Waterbury woman accused of holding her stepson captive for several decades pleaded not guilty in Waterbury Superior Court on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Musk to travel to Wisconsin ahead of judicial race, hand out $1M checks
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk announced late Thursday he would be traveling to Wisconsin ahead of a high-stakes state Supreme Court race to talk with voters and hand out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KRON4

item.title: Lawyer for Michigan couple held in Mexican prison says resort company is at fault
item.description: A Michigan couple remains behind bars in a Mexican prison, accused of breaching a timeshare contract — but their lawyer said the resort that filed the criminal complaint against them breached the contract first.
item.dc:creator: Shajaka Shelton, KRON4

item.title: Americans’ outlook on economy becomes a bit more pessimistic: Poll
item.description: Around 57 percent of those earning under $50,000 said their financial situation is “bad,” while 39 percent argued it is “good,” the poll found.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump restricts federal research funding, a lifeblood for colleges
item.description: After decades of partnership with the U.S. government, colleges are facing new doubts about the future of their federal funding.
item.dc:creator: SHARON LURYE and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration moves to end union rights for many federal workers
item.description: The order targets agencies it says have a national security mission but many of the departments don’t have a strict national security connection.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump order targets 'divisive narratives' in Smithsonian museums
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday signed an executive order seeking to exert more control over what is displayed at Smithsonian museums and federal sites, citing what the White House called “divisive narratives” promoted by certain museums.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Skeletal remains in Fayetteville identified as man missing since 2023, police say
item.description: Police said the remains were identified as Jaylin Isaiah Dilday, who was reported missing in May 2023.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Sockol, NewsNation

item.title: Judge says heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional as states face mounting lawsuits
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A federal judge's rebuke of Texas housing prisoners in lockups without air conditioning stopped short of ordering a fix before summer in what has become of the country's biggest lawsuits over keeping prisoners safe during dangerous extreme heat. But U.S.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter detained by feds
item.description: A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who have expressed their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge: High heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional” but did not order the state to begin installing air conditioners.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: DC-bound Amtrak passenger may have exposed travelers to measles, health officials say
item.description: The passenger rode an Amtrak train in the Northeast last week before visiting an urgent care center in DC, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Family unknowingly had rare, valuable painting for 150 years
item.description: A painting hanging in a French family's living room turned out to be far more valuable than just another trinket or heirloom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5
